---
title: "Assessing the Likelihood of Neurodevelopmental Conditions After Birth with Oxytocin: A Living Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis"
status: "living"
update-cadence: "24 months"
next-update: "To be determined"
final-version: ""
analytical_framework: ["Mixed Methods", "Frequentist", "Bayesian"]
oxytocin:
  intervention: "Perinatal oxytocin exposure"
  assessment_method: null
  route: null
  dosage: null
population:
  status: null
  age_group: "Children"
  clinical_type: null
outcomes:
  clinical:
    - "Neurodevelopmental"
update-frequency: "24 months"
dois:
  preregistration: "10.XXXX/33333"
  preprint: "10.XXXX/XXXXX"
  publication: "10.XXXX/66666"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    toc-title: "On this page"
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 3
---

::::: project-hero-section
:::: project-hero-content
::: project-hero-title
Assessing the Likelihood of Neurodevelopmental Conditions After Birth with Oxytocin: A Living Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
:::

```{=html}
<div class="project-status project-status--living">
  <span class="status-dot status-dot--living" aria-hidden="true"></span>
  <span class="status-text">Living meta-analysis</span>
  <span class="status-separator" aria-hidden="true"></span>
  <span class="status-detail">Updates every 24 months</span>
  <span class="status-separator" aria-hidden="true"></span>
  <span class="status-detail">Next update: To be determined</span>
</div>
```

::::
:::::

:::::::: project-info-panel
::::::: project-info-grid
::: {.info-card .authors-card}
### Authors

```{=html}
<div id="author-list">
  <div id="visible-authors">
    <strong>Mai-Linn Moxnes</strong> (University of Oslo)<br>
    <strong>Kjersti M. Walle</strong> (University of Oslo)<br>
    <strong>Daniel S. Quintana</strong> (Department of Psychology, University of Oslo | KG Jebsen Centre for Neurodevelopmental Disorders, University of Oslo | NevSom, Department of Rare Disorders and Disabilities, Oslo University Hospital)
  </div>

  <div id="hidden-authors" style="display:none">
    <strong>Anne Kaasen</strong> (Department of Nursing and Health Promotion, Faculty of Health Sciences, Oslo Metropolitan University)<br>
    <strong>Elisabeth Braithwaite</strong> (Department of Psychology, Faculty of Health and Education, Manchester Metropolitan University)<br>
    <strong>Nicky Wright</strong> (Department of Psychology, Faculty of Health and Education, Manchester Metropolitan University)<br>
    <strong>Ingebj&oslash;rg Iversen</strong> (Department of Psychology, University of Oslo)<br>
    <strong>Elisabeth Deilhaug</strong> (KG Jebsen Centre for Neurodevelopmental Disorders, University of Oslo | NevSom, Department of Rare Disorders and Disabilities, Oslo University Hospital)<br>
    <strong>Alina I. Sartorius</strong> (Department of Psychology, University of Oslo)<br>
    <strong>Heemin Kang</strong> (Department of Psychology, University of Oslo)<br>
    <strong>Terje N&aelig;rland</strong> (Department of Psychology, University of Oslo | KG Jebsen Centre for Neurodevelopmental Disorders, University of Oslo | NevSom, Department of Rare Disorders and Disabilities, Oslo University Hospital)<br>
    <strong>Ole A. Andreassen</strong> (KG Jebsen Centre for Neurodevelopmental Disorders, University of Oslo | Centre for Precision Psychiatry, Division of Mental Health and Addiction, University of Oslo and Oslo University Hospital)<br>
    <strong>Lars T. Westlye</strong> (KG Jebsen Centre for Neurodevelopmental Disorders, University of Oslo)<br>
    <strong>Mona Bekkhus</strong> (PROMENTA Research Center, Department of Psychology, University of Oslo)<br>
    <strong>Nicole N. L&oslash;nfeldt</strong> (Child and Adolescent Mental Health Center, Copenhagen University Hospital)<br>
  </div>

  <a href="#" id="author-toggle" class="toggle-link" onclick="toggleAuthors(); return false;">+ Show more authors</a>

  <script>
  function toggleAuthors() {
    var hiddenAuthors = document.getElementById("hidden-authors");
    var toggleButton = document.getElementById("author-toggle");

    if (hiddenAuthors.style.display === "none") {
      hiddenAuthors.style.display = "block";
      toggleButton.textContent = "- Show fewer authors";
    } else {
      hiddenAuthors.style.display = "none";
      toggleButton.textContent = "+ Show more authors";
    }
  }
  </script>
</div>
```
:::

::: {.info-card .timeline-card}
### Project

**Pre-registered:** February 04, 2025\
**Published:** Pending\
**Next update:** To be determined
:::

::: {.info-card .identifiers-card}
### Identifiers

**DOI:** <a href="https://osf.io/preprints/osf/9gx6s_v1" target="_blank">osf.io/preprints/osf/9gx6s_v1</a>

**Keywords:** autism, ADHD, oxytocin, meta-analysis, systematic review
:::

::: {.info-card .contact-card}
### Contact

**Principal Investigator:** Daniel S. Quintana\
**Email:** [daniel.quintana\@psykologi.uio.no](mailto:daniel.quintana@psykologi.uio.no)
:::
:::::::
::::::::

:::::: main-content-container
## Abstract {#abstract}

This living meta-analysis investigates the relationship between perinatal exposure to exogenous oxytocin and the likelihood of neurodevelopmental conditions such as attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and autism in children. Oxytocin is commonly used to induce and augment labor, with up to 50% of births in developed countries and varying rates (0.7% to 97.0%) in low- and lower-middle-income countries associated with synthetic oxytocin use.

The analysis employs a frequentist approach, utilizing random effects models with the Paule-Mandel estimator to synthesize findings across the literature. The project addresses whether perinatal exposure to exogenous oxytocin increases the likelihood of neurodevelopmental conditions in children. This living meta-analysis will be updated every 24 months, ensuring the incorporation of emerging evidence.

## Current Results {#current-results}

```{=html}
<div class="current-results-container">
    <div class="preregistered-notice">
        <p><strong>This is a pre-registered protocol.</strong> Initial analysis has not yet been conducted. Results will be posted when the analysis is completed and the pre-print is published.</p>

        <p>This living meta-analysis will provide updated evidence on the relationship between perinatal oxytocin exposure and neurodevelopmental outcomes, incorporating the latest research findings as they become available.</p>
    </div>

    <div class="previous-research">
        <div class="previous-research-title">Previous Research Context</div>
        <div class="previous-research-content">
            A previous meta-analysis by L&oslash;nfeldt and colleagues (2019) concluded that there was no evidence for an association between perinatal exogenous oxytocin exposure and the later diagnosis of ADHD and only marginal evidence for an association with autism. This living meta-analysis will update those findings with new research and provide ongoing evidence synthesis.
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
```

## Version History {#version-history}

```{=html}
<div class="version-history">

  <!-- v1: Pre-registration — expanded by default -->
  <div class="version-entry version-latest" data-version="1">
    <button class="version-header" aria-expanded="true" aria-controls="version-1-body">
      <div class="version-header-left">
        <span class="version-badge">v1</span>
        <div class="version-meta">
          <span class="version-label">Pre-registration</span>
          <span class="version-date">February 2025</span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="version-header-right">
        <span class="version-resource-count">1 resource</span>
        <svg class="version-chevron" width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" aria-hidden="true">
          <path fill-rule="evenodd" d="M5.23 7.21a.75.75 0 011.06.02L10 11.168l3.71-3.938a.75.75 0 111.08 1.04l-4.25 4.5a.75.75 0 01-1.08 0l-4.25-4.5a.75.75 0 01.02-1.06z" clip-rule="evenodd"/>
        </svg>
      </div>
    </button>

    <div class="version-body" id="version-1-body">
      <span class="version-authors-label">Version authors</span>
      <div class="version-authors">
        Moxnes, M.L., Walle, K.M., Kaasen, A., Braithwaite, E., Wright, N., Iversen, I., Deilhaug, E., Sartorius, A.I., Kang, H., N&aelig;rland, T., Andreassen, O.A., Westlye, L.T., Bekkhus, M., L&oslash;nfeldt, N.N., & Quintana, D.S.
      </div>

      <div class="version-resources-grid">
        <a href="https://osf.io/preprints/osf/9gx6s_v1" class="version-resource-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
          <svg class="version-resource-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" aria-hidden="true">
            <path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" d="M9 12h6m-6 4h6m2 5H7a2 2 0 01-2-2V5a2 2 0 012-2h5.586a1 1 0 01.707.293l5.414 5.414a1 1 0 01.293.707V19a2 2 0 01-2 2z"/>
          </svg>
          <span>Pre-registration</span>
        </a>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

</div>

<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  document.querySelectorAll('.version-header').forEach(function(header) {
    header.addEventListener('click', function() {
      var entry = this.closest('.version-entry');
      var body = entry.querySelector('.version-body');
      var isOpen = this.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true';
      this.setAttribute('aria-expanded', String(!isOpen));
      if (isOpen) {
        body.setAttribute('hidden', '');
      } else {
        body.removeAttribute('hidden');
      }
    });
  });
});
</script>
```

## Methodology {#methodology}

### Inclusion and Exclusion Criteria {#criteria}

::: criteria-box
#### Inclusion Criteria

-   **Type of Studies:** Original research written in English
-   **Exposure:** Perinatal synthetic oxytocin exposure
-   **Outcomes:** Any childhood neurodevelopmental outcome listed in DSM-5 or ICD-10 (including F-diagnoses and supplementary somatic diagnoses for delayed development: R41.8, R62, R62.0, R62.9)
-   **Outcomes:** Dimensional measures of psychiatric symptom severity
-   **Data Requirements:** Must report a risk estimate or data that would enable calculation of a risk estimate (e.g., frequencies or percentages)
:::

::: criteria-box
#### Exclusion Criteria

-   Studies that describe pharmacological induction without specifying the use of synthetic oxytocin
-   In cases of publications using the same sample and outcomes, later publications will be excluded
:::

### Search Strategy {#search-strategy}

::: search-string-box
[EMBASE:]{.search-db} `'Pitocin®' OR 'Syntocinon®' OR 'synthetic oxytocin' AND 'labour/labor' OR 'birth' OR 'perinatal' OR 'prenatal' OR 'obstetric'`

[Web of Science:]{.search-db} `('Pitocin®' OR 'Syntocinon®' OR 'synthetic oxytocin' AND 'labour/labor' OR 'birth' OR 'perinatal' OR 'prenatal' OR 'obstetric') AND ('delivery' AND 'augment*' OR 'induc*')`

[MEDLINE:]{.search-db} `'Pitocin®' OR 'Syntocinon®' OR 'synthetic oxytocin' AND 'labour/labor' OR 'birth' OR 'perinatal' OR 'prenatal' OR 'obstetric' AND 'antepartum'`
:::

### Related Research {#related-research}

This living meta-analysis builds on the previous meta-analysis by Lonfeldt and colleagues (2019), which concluded that there was no evidence for an association between perinatal exogenous oxytocin exposure and the later diagnosis of ADHD and only marginal evidence for an association with autism.

- [Lonfeldt et al. (2019) - Previous meta-analysis](https://doi.org/10.1017/S0033291718003021)
- [Stokholm et al. (2021) - Obstetric oxytocin exposure and ADHD/ASD](https://doi.org/10.1093/ije/dyaa076)

### Methodology Summary {#methodology-summary}

This living meta-analysis follows the PRISMA guidelines for systematic reviews. Our methodology is pre-registered and will incorporate data from the previous meta-analysis by Lonfeldt and colleagues (2019) as well as relevant data published since then.

The quality of included studies will be assessed using the modified Newcastle-Ottawa Quality Assessment Scales (NOS) for case-control and cohort studies. Studies will be independently rated by two reviewers within three categories: selection, comparability, and exposure/outcome, with scores categorized as low (0-3), moderate (4-7), or high (8-11) quality.

Statistical analyses will be conducted using R statistical software with the metafor, meta, and RoBMA packages. Random effects models will be used with the Paule-Mandel estimator for tau-squared. Heterogeneity will be evaluated by calculating I-squared and tau-squared. Publication bias will be assessed using Robust Bayesian Meta-Analysis (RoBMA) and by constructing contour-enhanced funnel plot visualization and performing Egger's regression test.

### Update Protocol {#update-protocol}

This is a living meta-analysis that will be updated every 24 months for a minimum of six years (three updates after initial publication). Updates will include:

1.  New database searches using the original search strategy
2.  Screening of new studies using identical inclusion/exclusion criteria
3.  Data extraction and quality assessment following pre-registered protocols
4.  Updated analysis incorporating all eligible studies
5.  Application of a conservative Bonferroni correction for four tests (p = 0.01 threshold for statistical significance)

## References {#references}

```{=html}
<div id="reference-list" style="font-size: 0.85rem;">
  <div id="visible-references">
    Bartos, F., Maier, M., Quintana, D. S., & Wagenmakers, E.-J. (2022). Adjusting for Publication Bias in JASP and R: Selection Models, PET-PEESE, and Robust Bayesian Meta-Analysis. <i>Advances in Methods and Practices in Psychological Science, 5</i>(3), 25152459221109259. https://doi.org/10.1177/25152459221109259<br><br>

    Egger, M., Smith, G. D., Schneider, M., & Minder, C. (1997). Bias in meta-analysis detected by a simple, graphical test. <i>BMJ (Clinical Research Ed.), 315</i>, 629-634. https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.315.7109.629<br><br>

    Elliott, J. H., Synnot, A., Turner, T., Simmonds, M., Akl, E. A., McDonald, S., et al. (2017). Living systematic review: 1. Introduction--the why, what, when, and how. <i>Journal of Clinical Epidemiology, 91</i>, 23-30. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jclinepi.2017.08.010<br><br>
  </div>

  <div id="hidden-references" style="display:none">
    Goffinet, F, Dreyfus, M, Carbonne, B, Magnin, G and Cabrol, D (2003). [Survey of the practice of cervical ripening and labor induction in France]. <i>Journal de Gyn&eacute;cologie Obst&eacute;trique et Biologie de la Reproduction, 32</i>, 638-646. https://europepmc.org/article/med/14699333<br><br>

    Kujabi, M. L., Mikkelsen, E., Housseine, N., Obel, J., et al. (2022). Labor augmentation with oxytocin in low- and lower-middle-income countries: A systematic review and meta-analysis. <i>AJOG Global Reports, 2</i>(4), 100123. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.xagr.2022.100123<br><br>

    L&oslash;nfeldt, N. N., Verhulst, F. C., Strandberg-Larsen, K., Plessen, K. J., & Lebowitz, E. R. (2019). Assessing risk of neurodevelopmental disorders after birth with oxytocin: A systematic review and meta-analysis. <i>Psychological Medicine, 49</i>(6), 881-890. https://doi.org/10.1017/S0033291718003021<br><br>

    Peters, J. L., Sutton, A. J., Jones, D. R., Abrams, K. R., & Rushton, L. (2008). Contour-enhanced meta-analysis funnel plots help distinguish publication bias from other causes of asymmetry. <i>Journal of Clinical Epidemiology, 61</i>, 991-996. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jclinepi.2007.11.010<br><br>

    Stokholm, L., Juhl, M., Talge, N. M., Gissler, M., Obel, C., & Strandberg-Larsen, K. (2021). Obstetric oxytocin exposure and ADHD and ASD among Danish and Finnish children. <i>International Journal of Epidemiology, 50</i>(2), 446-456. https://doi.org/10.1093/ije/dyaa076<br><br>

    Viechtbauer, W. (2010). Conducting Meta-Analyses in R with the metafor Package. <i>Journal of Statistical Software, 36</i>, 1-48. https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v036.i03
  </div>

  <a href="#" id="reference-toggle" class="toggle-link" onclick="toggleReferences(); return false;">+ Show more references</a>

  <script>
  function toggleReferences() {
    var hiddenReferences = document.getElementById("hidden-references");
    var toggleButton = document.getElementById("reference-toggle");

    if (hiddenReferences.style.display === "none") {
      hiddenReferences.style.display = "block";
      toggleButton.textContent = "- Show fewer references";
    } else {
      hiddenReferences.style.display = "none";
      toggleButton.textContent = "+ Show more references";
    }
  }
  </script>
</div>
```
::::::
